label: Penalty for Hindering or Attempting to Hinder Reporting
description: Member States must establish effective, proportionate and dissuasive penalties against persons who hinder or attempt to hinder reporting, including by obstructing the use of internal or external reporting channels.
amount_note: Amounts determined by Member States in national law. Some Member States have set maximum fines in the range of €10,000–€1,000,000 or equivalent.
trigger_conditions: Person takes active steps to prevent a reporting person from submitting a report
Person discourages a reporting person from submitting a report through threats, coercion, or intimidation

label: Penalty for Taking Retaliatory Measures Against Reporting Persons
description: Member States must establish effective, proportionate and dissuasive penalties against persons who take retaliatory measures against reporting persons, facilitators, or third parties connected with reporting persons.
amount_note: Determined by Member States. In addition to penalties, affected reporting persons are entitled to full compensation for all losses suffered, plus reinstatement where applicable.
trigger_conditions: Employer or any person in a work-related context takes any adverse action listed in Article 19 against a reporting person following their report

label: Penalty for Bringing Vexatious Proceedings Against Reporting Persons
description: Member States must establish penalties against persons who bring vexatious proceedings against reporting persons, including abusive civil lawsuits intended to intimidate or punish them for reporting.
amount_note: Determined by Member States. Anti-SLAPP measures may also require courts to dismiss clearly unfounded cases early in proceedings.
trigger_conditions: Legal proceedings are initiated against a reporting person in bad faith or as retaliation for their report
Proceedings are manifestly unfounded and intended to silence, harass, or punish the reporting person

label: Penalty for Breaching Duty of Maintaining Reporting Person's Confidentiality
description: Member States must establish penalties against persons who breach the duty of maintaining confidentiality of the reporting person's identity without their explicit consent.
amount_note: Determined by Member States. In practice, breach of confidentiality may also constitute a GDPR violation, exposing the entity to additional fines under GDPR enforcement.
trigger_conditions: Identity of the reporting person is disclosed without their consent to persons beyond authorised staff
Any other identifying information is disclosed that could directly or indirectly identify the reporting person

label: Penalty for Knowingly Making False Reports
description: Member States must lay down effective, proportionate and dissuasive penalties against persons who knowingly report or publicly disclose false information. Concerned persons who suffered harm as a result of false reports have the right to seek compensation.
amount_note: Determined by Member States. Does not apply to reports made in good faith based on reasonable belief, even if later found to be incorrect.
trigger_conditions: Reporting person knowingly submits a report containing false information
Reporting person knowingly makes a public disclosure of false information

label: Consequence for Failure to Establish Mandatory Internal Reporting Channel
description: Member States must ensure that failure by obligated entities to establish internal reporting channels within the required timeline results in effective enforcement action. The Directive does not specify a fixed penalty but requires Member States to provide effective, proportionate and dissuasive sanctions.
amount_note: Determined by Member States in their national transposition laws. Some Member States have established fines of up to €50,000 per violation.
trigger_conditions: Obligated entity with 50+ workers fails to establish or maintain an internal reporting channel
Obligated entity fails to designate a competent person to handle reports
Obligated entity fails to follow up on reports or provide feedback within 3 months
human_review_note: Specific penalty amounts for failure to establish channels are not set in the Directive itself but are determined by Member States. The €50,000 figure cited in some sources is illustrative of one Member State's implementation. Review applicable national law.

label: EU Commission Infringement Proceedings for Non-Transposition
description: Member States that failed to transpose the Directive by 17 December 2021 (or 17 December 2023 for SME obligations) may face infringement proceedings by the European Commission, which can result in financial penalties payable to the EU.
amount_note: EU infringement fines are calculated based on the severity of the breach and duration of non-compliance. The Commission has already initiated proceedings against non-compliant Member States.
trigger_conditions: Member State fails to notify transposition measures by the applicable deadline
Member State's transposition is incomplete or incorrect
